idea: build a birdseye-view retro dungen-crawler RPG game with map being generated as the play progresses

dimensions.title: Rendering Backend

options.label: DOM/CSS character grid
HTML5 Canvas with JS-drawn glyphs

dimensions.cost_note: option b is roughly 10x more rendering code: manual glyph metrics, redraw loops, and HUD composition in JS — no user-visible gain for a text-only game

dimensions.title: Player Leveling Mechanic

options.label: Level = floor number (display only, no XP system)
XP from kills, level-up grants stat boost

dimensions.cost_note: option b requires inventing XP values, level thresholds, stat-scaling tables, and balance passes — estimated 2–3x scope expansion on the character system

options.label: 3 tiles (tight / claustrophobic)
5 tiles (balanced / standard roguelike)
7 tiles (wide / tactical)

dimensions.title: Map Generation Algorithm

options.label: BSP partitioning (rectangular rooms, connectivity by construction)
Random room placement with nearest-neighbor tunnels
Drunkard's walk / cellular automata (organic caves)

dimensions.cost_note: option c requires a flood-fill connectivity pass after each generation step to satisfy FR-013 — risks generation stalls on bad seeds

options.label: Step on staircase tile on floor 10 → victory screen (no floor 11)
Eliminate all enemies on floor 10 → victory screen
Entering floor 10 is itself the win condition
HP bar changes only (numeric, no text)
One-line HUD log per event ('You hit Goblin for 4. Goblin hits for 2.')
Green-on-black (classic terminal)
Amber-on-black (old CRT)
Limited color by entity type (white/red/yellow on black)
White-on-black (print style)

dimensions.title: Enemy Engagement Rule

options.label: Adjacency-only: combat on move-onto-adjacent-tile
Room-wide aggro: all room enemies activate on player entry

dimensions.cost_note: option b requires each enemy to track room membership at all times, adding entity state not present in the current entity model
